limitations: This certificate is restricted to the committed post-K8 free-subtracted, mass-tuned Schwinger interaction-delta tail obligation and does not claim exact QFT success.
The certificate does not prove OS reconstruction, a full Schwinger-family theorem, infinite-volume physics, or removal of every regulator beyond this narrow post-K8 tail obligation.
K and N remain temporary regulators outside this certified post-K8 tail-ratio contract.

operator_tail_derivation.derivation_role: closed proof envelope deriving the effective tail ratio from lower-level Hamiltonian insertion, counterterm Lipschitz, resolvent-gap, and occupation-support lemmas; the canonical theorem is closed and has no implication hypothesis

primitive_bounds.name: high_k_phi0_matrix_element_bound_after_K8
primitive_bounds.proved_from: finite zero-total-momentum Hamiltonian high-mode matrix-element envelope

primitive_bounds.name: occupation_tail_support_after_K8
primitive_bounds.proved_from: K6/N5 support margin carried only as a finite-sector support lemma inside this narrow envelope

proof_steps.claim: post-K8 free-subtracted Hamiltonian high-mode insertion bound
proof_steps.justification: The formal artifact closes the high_k_phi0_matrix_element_bound_after_K8 lemma from primitive Hamiltonian matrix-element envelope constants rather than from an external theorem hypothesis.
The formal artifact closes the mass_counterterm_lipschitz_bound_after_K8 lemma and combines it with the high-mode insertion bound.

The formal artifact proves uniform_high_K_operator_counterterm_estimate_after_K8 from the closed Hamiltonian and counterterm lemmas plus occupation-tail support, producing ratio numerator 7 denominator 10.
The formal artifact derives the effective tail ratio and the geometric remainder inequality 0.00020360279597723494 <= 0.0002097140644003015.

theorem_statements.name: high_k_phi0_matrix_element_bound_after_K8
theorem_statements.scope: lower-level Hamiltonian field operator control for the post-K8 Schwinger free-subtracted mass-tuned sector
theorem_statements.statement: The normal-ordered high-K phi(0) matrix-element contribution in the zero-total-momentum Fock Hamiltonian is bounded by the explicit post-K8 geometric weight used for the free-subtracted Schwinger delta.

theorem_statements.name: mass_counterterm_lipschitz_bound_after_K8
theorem_statements.scope: lower-level mass-tuning counterterm control for the post-K8 Schwinger free-subtracted mass-tuned sector
theorem_statements.statement: The finite-cutoff mass counterterm shift is Lipschitz in the post-K8 Hamiltonian increment with constant included in the 0.7 effective contraction.

theorem_statements.name: uniform_high_K_operator_counterterm_estimate_after_K8
theorem_statements.scope: post-K8 Schwinger free-subtracted mass-tuned operator and counterterm tail control
theorem_statements.statement: Combining the Hamiltonian high-K phi(0) matrix-element bound, counterterm Lipschitz bound, occupation-tail support, and resolvent gap bound proves a uniform post-K8 effective tail ratio 0.7, below required 0.7061735719977987.

theorem_statements.name: effective_tail_ratio_from_uniform_high_K_operator_counterterm_estimate
theorem_statements.scope: post-K8 Schwinger free-subtracted mass-tuned tail contraction
theorem_statements.statement: The proved uniform high-K operator/counterterm estimate derives the post-K8 effective tail ratio 0.7 for the committed free-subtracted, mass-tuned Schwinger remainder.

theorem_statements.name: post_k8_schwinger_free_subtracted_mass_tuned_remainder_bound
theorem_statements.scope: post-K8 Schwinger free-subtracted mass-tuned remainder bound
theorem_statements.statement: The derived post-K8 free-subtracted, mass-tuned Schwinger remainder bound 0.00020360279597723494 is less than or equal to target_tail_bound 0.0002097140644003015.
